zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Stewart於1400年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾時，她的父親Robert Stewart 1st Lord Lorn14歲，母親Lady Joan Stewart of Albany21歲。她於1419年與William Douglas 2nd Lord of Cavers在蘇格蘭阿吉爾奧陳布雷可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1432年8月1日在奧地利波麥其洛霍福享年32歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾蒙。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Stewart was born in 1400, in Argyll, Scotland, her father, Robert Stewart 1st Lord Lorn, was 14 and her mother, Lady Joan Stewart of Albany, was 21. She married William Douglas 2nd Lord of Cavers in 1419, in Auchenbreck, Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 1 August 1432, in Lochow, Jičin, Bohemia, Austria, at the age of 32, and was buried in Kilmun, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Stewart est née en 1400, à Argyll, Écosse, son père, Robert Stewart 1st Lord Lorn, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Stewart of Albany, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé William Douglas 2nd Lord of Cavers en 1419, à Auchenbreck, Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1432, à Lochow, Jičin, Bohême, Autriche, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilmun, Argyll, Écosse.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stewart(이)가 1400년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Stewart 1st Lord Lorn(은)는 14세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Stewart of Albany(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1419년 스컷랜드 아그이 오천브레크에서 William Douglas 2nd Lord of Cavers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1432년 8월 1일 오스트리아 브oh멘 지c인 럭에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬먼에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Stewart родилась в 1400 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert Stewart 1st Lord Lorn было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan Stewart of Albany, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Douglas 2nd Lord of Cavers в 1419 г. в местности аучэнбрекк, аргилл, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 августа 1432 г. в местности лочоу, цзиcин, бoхмен, Австрия в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности килмун, аргилл, Шотландия.
